summary: Follow-up hygiene discovered while converting direct-script ImportError fallbacks to explicit package-context branches for mypy compatibility.

observations.problem: The direct-script fallback migration removes the no-redef import noise, but broad mypy still exposes concrete typing errors in runtime modules.
observations.why_it_matters: Expanding MYPY_TARGETS before these real type errors are fixed would turn the allowlist hard gate into a noisy migration gate instead of a reliable static quality layer.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a small per-module mypy expansion report that runs mypy on candidate modules, groups remaining errors by error code, and only proposes MYPY_TARGETS additions once the candidate module is green.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-19 by fixing the representative concrete mypy errors, then clearing the remaining broad ops/scripts blockers in auto_improve_runtime.py, codex_exec_executor.py, mechanism_review_candidate_runtime.py, mechanism_review_history_runtime.py, mechanism_run_validation_runtime.py, planning_gate_validate_runtime.py, raw_registry_runtime.py, and wiki_page_runtime.py. MYPY_TARGETS now points at ops/mypy-allowlist.txt, which covers the full ops/scripts/*.py surface.

observations.surface: pyproject.toml, ops/direct-script-entrypoints.txt, ops/scripts/*.py, README.md, ops/README.md, tests/test_import_fallback_contract.py, tests/test_script_module_surface_contract.py
observations.problem: The first migration preserves direct-script compatibility across 76 files, including runtime/helper modules that are not documented direct-script entrypoints.
observations.why_it_matters: The final import contract should keep direct-script compatibility only where it is intentionally supported; otherwise duplicated import branches remain a maintenance burden.
observations.automation_candidate: Derive or maintain an explicit direct-script entrypoint allowlist and add a test that rejects __package__ fallback branches outside that allowlist.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-19 by adding ops/direct-script-entrypoints.txt, locking tests to reject direct-script branches outside that allowlist, converting allowlisted entrypoints to sys.path repo-root bootstrap plus ops.scripts absolute imports for direct execution, and removing fallback branches from non-entrypoint runtime/helper modules. Executor CLI was split by adding ops/scripts/executor.py as the thin direct-script wrapper, moving auto-improve execution to ops.scripts.executor, and removing the direct-script branch from executor_runtime.py. Extended on 2026-04-23 by adding canonical llm-wiki-* console scripts in pyproject.toml, marking direct scripts as transitional wrappers, and testing that project.scripts exactly covers the 42 direct wrapper targets.
